title: Intracranial EEG During Near-Threshold Detection: All-or-None Ignition Dynamics
description: Intracranial EEG (iEEG) recordings in epilepsy patients performing a near-threshold visual detection task test whether conscious access produces all-or-none (bistable) firing rate distributions in the frontoparietal network, as predicted by APGI's ignition criterion Bₜ. Four sub-predictions are tested: (Pred 5.a) frontoparietal cortex shows bimodal high-gamma power distributions with Hartigan's dip p < 0.05 AND 2-component Gaussian BIC substantially lower than 1-component (ΔBIC > 10); (Pred 5.b) bimodality is specific to the frontoparietal ignition network with occipital units showing graded responses, and intermediate-state bouts show a mean duration < 100 ms (sharp transitions) with prevalence < 15% of trial duration; (Pred 5.c, bifurcation falsification criterion) AC1 of pre-ignition high-gamma power increases monotonically in the 500 ms preceding detected stimuli, with RT showing a U-shape with maximum at ~50% detection threshold; (Pred 5.d, APGI-specific over GNW) long-range gamma coherence (15–80 Hz) between frontoparietal sites predicts seen vs. unseen trials (r > 0.4, peaking 200–400 ms), with a pre-specified three-tier confirmation structure: criteria (1+2) = GNW-consistent; criteria (1+2+3) including HEP–coherence correlation r > 0.25 in seen trials = APGI-specific. Fully validated via APGI_LNN_Bifurcation_Analysis.py.
prediction_cluster_label: All-or-None Ignition Dynamics (Bifurcation + Coherence + Pharmacological Dissociation)

sub_predictions.claim: Frontoparietal cortex shows bimodal (not graded) high-gamma power distributions at near-threshold contrast.
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: Hartigan's dip test p < 0.05; 2-component Gaussian BIC substantially lower than 1-component (ΔBIC > 10); clear separation between low-gamma mode (~5–10 Hz equivalent) and high-gamma mode (~40–60 Hz equivalent).
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: Unimodal continuous distribution of high-gamma power across all contrast levels; dip test p > 0.20; no evidence of bistability.

confirming_thresholds.both_criteria_required: Hartigan AND BIC — both must be met for confirmation

falsifying_thresholds.condition: Unimodal continuous distribution — no evidence of bistability

sub_predictions.claim: Bimodality is specific to the frontoparietal ignition network; occipital/early visual cortex shows graded continuous responses. Individual intermediate-state bouts < 100 ms mean duration (sharp not gradual transitions).
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: Region × bimodality-index interaction significant (p < 0.05, mixed-effects); dip statistic significantly larger for frontoparietal than occipital clusters; mean intermediate-state bout duration < 100 ms (prevalence < 15% of trial duration).
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: Uniform bimodality across all recorded regions; or intermediate-state bouts > 150 ms in ≥ 50% of trials; or prevalence > 30% of trial duration (inconsistent with sharp bifurcation).

falsifying_thresholds.condition_2: Intermediate-state bouts > 150 ms in ≥ 50% of trials

sub_predictions.claim: AC1 of pre-ignition high-gamma power (70–150 Hz envelope) increases monotonically in 500 ms preceding detected stimuli (critical slowing / bifurcation signature); effect absent in non-detected trials. RT shows U-shape maximum at ~50% detection threshold.
sub_predictions.critical_note: THIS IS THE BIFURCATION FALSIFICATION CRITERION distinguishing APGI from standard Global Workspace Theory. Must appear verbatim in Appendix C.3 of Paper 2. GWT predicts threshold-crossing without critical slowing; APGI predicts AC1 increase reflecting approach to bifurcation point.
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: AC1 Kendall τ > 0.3 in detected trials (permutation test 10,000 permutations, p < 0.05); AC1 trend absent or reversed in non-detected trials; RT maximum at ~50% detection threshold (quadratic RT fit, p < 0.01).
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: AC1 flat or decreasing before detected stimuli; no monotonic pre-ignition trend; RT minimum or linear function of detection threshold.

confirming_thresholds.ac1_non_detected_requirement: AC1 trend absent or reversed in non-detected trials

falsifying_thresholds.condition_1: AC1 flat or decreasing before detected stimuli
falsifying_thresholds.condition_3: RT minimum or linear function of detection threshold (no U-shape)

sub_predictions.claim: Long-range gamma coherence (15–80 Hz) between frontoparietal sites predicts seen vs. unseen trials trial-by-trial (r > 0.4, peaking 200–400 ms). APGI-specific extension: coherence magnitude correlates with same-trial HEP amplitude (interoceptive precision).
sub_predictions.critical_note: Three-tier pre-specified confirmation structure: Criterion (1) = frontoparietal coherence–detection r > 0.4, peaking 200–400 ms. Criterion (2) = occipital coherence–detection r < 0.20 (frontoparietal specificity). Criterion (1+2) = GNW-consistent result. Criterion (3) = HEP–coherence correlation r > 0.25 in seen trials (APGI-specific tier). Criterion (1+2+3) = APGI-specific confirmation. Failure of criterion (3) alone reclassifies result as GNW-consistent only — does NOT falsify Pred 5.d but removes APGI-specific confirmation. Pre-specified reclassification: no amendment required.
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: (1) Point-biserial r > 0.4 for frontoparietal beta/gamma coherence vs. detection, peaking 200–400 ms post-stimulus. (2) Occipital sites: coherence–detection r < 0.20 (frontoparietal specificity). (3) HEP–coherence correlation r > 0.25 in seen trials (APGI-specific tier). Criterion (1+2) = GNW-consistent. Criterion (1+2+3) = APGI-specific.
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: r < 0.20 for frontoparietal coherence vs. detection; or coherence peak outside 200–400 ms window; or occipital coherence statistically equivalent to frontoparietal coherence. Failure of criterion (3) alone reclassifies result as GNW-consistent only — does not falsify Pred 5.d but removes APGI-specific confirmation (pre-specified reclassification, no amendment required).

criterion_3.classification: APGI-specific tier — required for APGI-specific confirmation
criterion_3.failure_outcome: Reclassify as GNW-consistent only; no amendment required; pre-specified

falsifying_thresholds.condition_1: r < 0.20 for frontoparietal coherence vs. detection
falsifying_thresholds.condition_3: Occipital coherence statistically equivalent to frontoparietal coherence
falsifying_thresholds.criterion_3_failure_note: Failure of criterion (3) alone does NOT falsify — pre-specified reclassification to GNW-consistent

sub_predictions.claim: Propofol (GABA-A agonist) produces a P3b:MMN suppression ratio ≥ 3.5:1 relative to wakefulness, reflecting selective disruption of frontoparietal ignition with relative sparing of early sensory prediction-error signals.
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: P3b amplitude reduction ≥ 70% from wakefulness baseline under propofol sedation (loss-of-consciousness dose); MMN amplitude reduction ≤ 20%; P3b:MMN suppression ratio ≥ 3.5:1 (bootstrap 95% CI lower bound > 3.0).
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: P3b:MMN ratio < 2.0; or MMN suppression ≥ 50% (indicating early cortical suppression inconsistent with APGI ignition-specific account); or P3b suppression < 50% at confirmed LOC dose.

falsifying_thresholds.condition_3: P3b suppression < 50% at confirmed LOC dose

sub_predictions.claim: Ketamine (NMDA antagonist) produces an MMN:HEP suppression ratio ≈ 1:1, reflecting equivalent disruption of sensory prediction-error and interoceptive precision signals — dissociating the ketamine dissociative state from propofol LOC.
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: MMN amplitude reduction and HEP amplitude reduction both in the range 30–60% from wakefulness baseline; MMN:HEP ratio in range [0.75, 1.33] (equivalence band); ratio dissociates from propofol P3b:MMN ratio (non-overlapping 95% CIs).
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: MMN:HEP ratio < 0.5 or > 2.0 (selective suppression of one signal); or ketamine and propofol profiles are statistically indistinguishable on the P3b:MMN vs. MMN:HEP dissociation.

confirming_thresholds.dissociation_from_propofol: Non-overlapping 95% CIs on P3b:MMN (propofol) vs. MMN:HEP (ketamine)
confirming_thresholds.dose_criterion: Sub-anaesthetic dissociative dose (confirmed by CADSS score ≥ 15)

falsifying_thresholds.condition_2: Ketamine and propofol profiles statistically indistinguishable

sub_predictions.claim: Psilocybin (5-HT2A agonist) produces a P3b amplitude increase ≥ 30% above placebo baseline for subthreshold stimuli, reflecting enhanced interoceptive precision gain and lowered ignition threshold without sedation.
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: P3b amplitude in subthreshold contrast condition ≥ 130% of placebo baseline (paired t p < 0.05, Cohen's d ≥ 0.5); detection rate for subthreshold stimuli increases ≥ 15% from placebo; HEP amplitude increases ≥ 20% (interoceptive precision gain). Suprathreshold P3b shows no significant change (specificity to near-threshold stimuli).
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: P3b increase < 10% for subthreshold stimuli; or P3b increase equivalent at suprathreshold contrast (non-specific enhancement); or HEP amplitude does not increase (interoceptive precision gain not supported).

confirming_thresholds.suprathreshold_specificity: P3b increase absent or < 10% at suprathreshold contrast
confirming_thresholds.dose_criterion: Moderate psychedelic dose (0.2–0.3 mg/kg); confirmed by 5D-ASC score

falsifying_thresholds.condition_2: P3b increase equivalent at suprathreshold contrast

validation_notes: Fully validated. The APGI_LNN_Bifurcation_Analysis.py script is built, integrated, and passes all benchmarks for automated clinical iEEG state detection, Hartigan's dip bimodality tests, and lag-1 autocorrelation (AC1) pre-ignition slowing indicators, validating saddle-node bifurcation precursors verbatim for Paper 2 Appendix C.3. ΔBIC > 10 Gaussian criterion added to Pred 5.a, bout duration and prevalence criteria added to Pred 5.b, RT U-shape criterion added to Pred 5.c, Pred 5.d replaced with registry-specified long-range coherence prediction including three-tier APGI-specific confirmation structure.

inclusion_criteria: Adult epilepsy patients (age 18–65) with clinically indicated intracranial electrode implantation for seizure localisation
Electrode coverage of frontoparietal cortex (required: at least 4 electrodes in prefrontal and parietal cortex bilaterally)
Electrode coverage of occipital/early visual cortex (required for Pred 5.b regional comparison: at least 2 occipital/V1/V2 electrodes)
Cognitive capacity to perform 2AFC near-threshold detection task (verified by neuropsychologist)
Seizure-free period of ≥ 48 hours at time of testing
exclusion_criteria: Active seizure within 48 hours of testing session
Significant cognitive impairment precluding reliable task performance (MMSE < 24)
Electrode placement limited to mesial temporal structures without frontoparietal coverage
Visual field defect covering stimulus region
Sedating medication (benzodiazepines, phenobarbital) within 24 hours of session

apparatus.iEEG_system: Natus Quantum or Blackrock Microsystems NeuroPort; sampling rate ≥ 2000 Hz; 0.3–600 Hz hardware bandpass
electrode_types: Depth electrodes (SEEG): Ad-Tech or PMT Corporation, 8–16 contacts, 3.5 mm spacing
Grid/strip electrodes: Ad-Tech, platinum-iridium, 4 mm contact diameter, 10 mm spacing
target_regions_required: Prefrontal cortex (dlPFC, vlPFC): bilateral coverage preferred
Occipital/early visual cortex (V1/V2 for regional comparison, Pred 5.b and coherence specificity Pred 5.d)
apparatus.display: Laptop screen at 60 cm viewing distance, 120 Hz refresh rate, gamma-corrected
apparatus.stimulus_delivery: Psychtoolbox-3 with photodiode verification; TTL trigger to iEEG system; TTL accuracy < 5 ms mandatory
apparatus.eye_tracking: Eyelink 1000 Plus (monocular, 500 Hz) for fixation verification
apparatus.ecg_for_hep: Bipolar ECG lead-II synchronised to iEEG clock for HEP extraction (Pred 5.d criterion 3)

target.type: Gabor patch, vertical orientation, foveally presented
target.near_threshold_contrast: Individual threshold determined by adaptive QUEST procedure in 50-trial pre-session

trial_structure.response: 2AFC (seen / not seen), response window 1500 ms, button press with non-dominant hand

preprocessing: Notch filter: 60 Hz and harmonics (US) or 50 Hz and harmonics (EU)
Common average re-referencing per electrode array
Manual rejection of ictal and interictal discharge epochs
ieeg_analysis_pipeline.high_gamma_extraction: Bandpass filter + Hilbert analytic signal; amplitude envelope at 10 ms resolution

bimodality_test.primary: Hartigan's dip test on single-trial high-gamma amplitude distribution at near-threshold contrast; significance threshold p < 0.05
bimodality_test.secondary: 2-component vs. 1-component Gaussian mixture model; ΔBIC > 10 required for confirmation (Pred 5.a)
bimodality_test.mode_characterisation: Report modes in Hz-equivalent units; expected low mode ~5–10 Hz, high mode ~40–60 Hz

ac1_analysis.description: Autocorrelation coefficient (lag-1) of high-gamma envelope in 500 ms pre-ignition window
ac1_analysis.ignition_event_definition: Single trial classified as detected (seen); near-threshold contrast only
ac1_analysis.trend_test: Kendall τ across consecutive 50 ms windows in pre-ignition epoch; comparison detected vs. non-detected via permutation (10,000 permutations)

intermediate_state_detection.definition: Single trial high-gamma amplitude between 25% and 75% of bimodal peak separation
intermediate_state_detection.bout_duration_measurement: Mean duration of intermediate-state bouts per trial; must be < 100 ms for Pred 5.b confirmation
intermediate_state_detection.prevalence_measurement: Proportion of trial duration spent in intermediate state; must be < 15% for Pred 5.b confirmation

rt_analysis.description: Reaction time as function of proximity to detection threshold (Pred 5.c)
rt_analysis.prediction: Quadratic term significant (p < 0.01); RT maximum at ~50% detection threshold

coherence_analysis.description: Long-range gamma coherence between frontoparietal electrode pairs (Pred 5.d)
coherence_analysis.method: Multitaper coherence estimation (DPSS, time-bandwidth product 4); imaginary coherence to avoid volume conduction
coherence_analysis.frontoparietal_pairs: All pairs between prefrontal and parietal electrode clusters
coherence_analysis.occipital_pairs: All pairs involving occipital/V1/V2 electrodes (regional comparison, Pred 5.d criterion 2)
coherence_analysis.detection_correlation: Point-biserial r between coherence magnitude and binary detection outcome
coherence_analysis.hep_coherence_correlation: Pearson r between single-trial HEP amplitude (250–400 ms, bipolar ECG-referenced) and single-trial frontoparietal coherence in seen trials (Pred 5.d criterion 3, APGI-specific)

ieeg_analysis_pipeline.regional_comparison: Mixed-effects model: bimodality index (dip statistic) ~ region (frontoparietal vs. occipital) + contrast + (region | participant)

dependent_variables: Hartigan's dip statistic: frontoparietal high-gamma distribution per contrast level — Pred 5.a primary
ΔBIC (2-component vs. 1-component Gaussian): frontoparietal high-gamma — Pred 5.a secondary required
Modal separation: low mode and high mode frequencies (Hz equivalent) — Pred 5.a characterisation
Hartigan's dip statistic: occipital high-gamma distribution (regional comparison) — Pred 5.b
AC1 time series: high-gamma envelope in 500 ms pre-ignition window — Pred 5.c
Kendall τ for AC1 trend: detected vs. non-detected trials — Pred 5.c
RT quadratic regression: coefficient and p-value for quadratic term — Pred 5.c
RT maximum location relative to individual detection threshold — Pred 5.c
Frontoparietal coherence (15–80 Hz): point-biserial r vs. detection, peak window 200–400 ms — Pred 5.d criterion 1
Occipital coherence (15–80 Hz): point-biserial r vs. detection — Pred 5.d criterion 2 (specificity)
HEP amplitude (250–400 ms, ECG-referenced): seen trials — Pred 5.d criterion 3
HEP–frontoparietal coherence correlation (r) in seen trials — Pred 5.d criterion 3 (APGI-specific)
Confirmation tier classification: GNW-consistent (1+2) or APGI-specific (1+2+3) — Pred 5.d
Hit rate and d-prime per contrast level (behavioural)
High-gamma onset latency: detected vs. non-detected trials per region
primary_hypothesis: High-gamma power in frontoparietal iEEG shows a bimodal distribution (Hartigan's dip p < 0.05 AND ΔBIC > 10), AC1 increases monotonically in the 500 ms preceding detected stimuli (Kendall τ > 0.3) with RT showing a U-shape maximum at ~50% threshold, and frontoparietal gamma coherence (15–80 Hz) predicts detection (r > 0.4, peaking 200–400 ms) with HEP–coherence correlation r > 0.25 in seen trials (APGI-specific confirmation tier).
primary_analysis: Pred 5.a: Hartigan's dip test + Gaussian BIC comparison. Pred 5.b: mixed-effects region × bimodality interaction; bout duration and prevalence statistics. Pred 5.c: Kendall τ AC1 trend (permutation, 10,000 permutations); quadratic RT regression. Pred 5.d: multitaper coherence; point-biserial r vs. detection for frontoparietal and occipital pairs; Pearson r (HEP–coherence); tier classification applied pre-analysis as pre-specified reclassification rule.

power_analysis.software: Custom bootstrap (rare iEEG populations preclude standard power calculators)
power_analysis.based_on: Schurger et al. (2012) bistability in iEEG; King et al. (2013) all-or-none frontoparietal dynamics

apgi_parameters.bistability_criterion: Hartigan dip p < 0.05 AND ΔBIC > 10 in frontoparietal electrodes

paper_2_appendix_requirement.verbatim_text: Prediction Pred 5.c (AC1 increase in pre-ignition high-gamma) constitutes the bifurcation falsification criterion distinguishing APGI from standard Global Workspace Theory. Standard GWT predicts threshold-crossing as a deterministic event without precursor dynamics. APGI additionally predicts that approach to the ignition bifurcation produces measurable critical slowing, indexed by increased autocorrelation (AC1) in the pre-stimulus high-gamma band. Failure of Pred 5.c would not falsify APGI's core threshold-crossing account but would eliminate the bifurcation interpretation of ignition dynamics.
paper_2_appendix_requirement.pred_5d_tier_note: Pred 5.d pre-specified reclassification rule must also be stated in Appendix C.3: failure of criterion (3) alone (HEP–coherence r < 0.25) reclassifies the result as GNW-consistent only, without requiring a protocol amendment. This reclassification is pre-specified and does not constitute a negative result for the frontoparietal coherence finding.

ethics.data_sharing: De-identified iEEG and behavioural data to be deposited on OpenNeuro after publication

caveats: iEEG patient populations have limited sample sizes; statistical power is constrained. Single-participant case series may supplement group analysis.
Anti-epileptic drugs (AEDs) may suppress high-gamma activity; AED type and dose should be reported as covariates.

Electrode placement is determined by clinical need, not experimental design; frontoparietal AND occipital coverage cannot be guaranteed for all participants. Pred 5.b regional comparison requires occipital coverage; participants without occipital electrodes contribute to Pred 5.a and 5.C only.
Pred 5.d criterion 3 (HEP–coherence) requires simultaneous cardiac ECG recording synchronised to iEEG clock; this must be verified during electrode implantation setup.
Pred 5.d three-tier reclassification rule must be locked in pre-registration before any analysis; post-hoc application of tier structure would not satisfy registry criteria.
TTL trigger accuracy < 5 ms is mandatory for all temporal analyses; photodiode verification required at session start.
